# lauren-mcp

> Model Context Protocol (MCP) server and client for the Lauren web framework.

## What is lauren-mcp?

`lauren-mcp` extends the Lauren Python web framework with support for the Model Context
Protocol. It provides two capabilities:

**Server**: Expose any Lauren service as an MCP server. AI clients (Claude, custom
agents, etc.) can discover and call your service's tools over WebSocket or HTTP+SSE.

**Client**: Connect to any remote MCP server (stdio, WebSocket, HTTP+SSE) and wire its
tools into a Lauren AI agent with automatic namespacing.

## Installation

```
pip install lauren-mcp               # core: wire types + server decorators + stdio client
pip install "lauren-mcp[ws]"         # + WebSocket client (requires websockets)
pip install "lauren-mcp[http]"       # + HTTP+SSE client (requires httpx + httpx-sse)
pip install "lauren-mcp[all]"        # all transports
```

## Key concepts

- `@mcp_server(path, *, transport="ws")` ŌĆö register a class as an MCP server
- `@mcp_tool()` ŌĆö expose an async method as an MCP tool (JSON Schema from type hints)
- `@mcp_resource(uri_template)` ŌĆö expose a URI-addressable resource
- `@mcp_prompt()` ŌĆö expose a parameterised prompt template
- `McpServerModule.for_root(server_cls, *, transport="ws")` ŌĆö build a Lauren `@module`
- `McpServer.stdio/ws/http` ŌĆö factory methods returning `McpClientProtocol`
- `McpCallError` ŌĆö raised when the server returns a JSON-RPC error response
- `McpServerConfig(alias, client)` ŌĆö config for one remote MCP server
- `McpToolBridge([configs])` ŌĆö lifecycle manager for a list of McpServerConfig
- Tool namespacing: `{alias}__{tool_name}` prevents collisions

## Wire protocol

JSON-RPC 2.0 over three transports:

| Transport | Extra | Endpoint path |
|---|---|---|
| stdio | (none) | subprocess stdin/stdout |
| WebSocket | `[ws]` | `{path}/ws` |
| HTTP+SSE | `[http]` | `{path}` (GET = SSE stream, POST = messages) |

## Public API (`__all__`)

Server: `mcp_server`, `mcp_tool`, `mcp_resource`, `mcp_prompt`, `McpServerModule`

Client: `McpServer`, `McpClientProtocol`, `McpCallError`, `McpServerConfig`,
`McpToolBridge`

Wire types: `JsonRpcRequest`, `JsonRpcNotification`, `JsonRpcResponse`,
`JsonRpcErrorResponse`, `McpErrorCode`, `McpParseError`,
`parse_message`, `build_error_response`,
`ToolSchema`, `ResourceSchema`, `PromptSchema`,
`TextContent`, `ImageContent`, `EmbeddedResource`, `AnyContent`,
`PromptArgument`, `PromptMessage`,
`InitializeParams`, `InitializeResult`,
`ClientCapabilities`, `ServerCapabilities`, `Implementation`,
`ToolCallParams`, `ToolResult`,
`ReadResourceParams`, `ReadResourceResult`,
`GetPromptParams`, `GetPromptResult`

Version constants: `LATEST`, `STABLE`, `SUPPORTED`

## Using with Lauren

```python
from lauren import LaurenFactory, module
from lauren_mcp import mcp_server, mcp_tool, McpServerModule

@mcp_server("/mcp")
class MyServer:
    @mcp_tool()
    async def greet(self, name: str) -> str:
        """Greet someone. Args: name: Person's name."""
        return f"Hello, {name}!"

@module(imports=[McpServerModule.for_root(MyServer)])
class AppModule:
    pass

app = LaurenFactory.create(AppModule)
# WebSocket at ws://host/mcp/ws
```

## Connecting a client

```python
import asyncio
from lauren_mcp import McpServer, McpCallError

async def main():
    client = McpServer.stdio(["python", "-m", "my_server"])
    await client.connect()
    tools = await client.list_tools()
    result = await client.call_tool("greet", {"name": "World"})
    print(result["content"][0]["text"])  # raw dict return
    await client.close()

asyncio.run(main())
```

## Links

- Documentation: https://mcp.lauren-py.dev
- Source: https://github.com/lauren-framework/lauren-mcp
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/lauren-mcp/
- llms-full.txt: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lauren-framework/lauren-mcp/refs/heads/main/llms-full.txt
